content: Add a new isCommunityWorkout boolean field to the planned workout model. This field marks workouts created by the Amp customer community. On the iOS app, community workouts should display a unique icon/badge on the workout preview screen to distinguish them from coach-created workouts. This is a cross-repo feature: backend schema + iOS UI.

content.text: I'll start by researching both repos to understand the current planned workout model and workout preview screen, then plan the implementation.

input.title: Add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout model
input.summary: Researching backend schema for planned workout model and iOS workout preview screen

input.description:
Research the planned workout model in the RedefinedFitness backend repo. I need to understand:

1. Find the GraphQL schema definition for PlannedWorkout (search in `amplify/backend/api/` or similar). Show me the full type definition.
2. Find the database schema/model for PlannedWorkout (search in packages that handle DB, like drizzle or prisma schemas). Show me the table definition.
3. Find any resolvers that return PlannedWorkout data (Lambda resolvers, AppSync resolvers, etc.)
4. Find the `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` file and search for PlannedWorkout type definition there.
5. Check if there's an existing pattern for boolean fields on PlannedWorkout (like isPublic, isActive, etc.) that I can follow.

Return:
- The exact file paths and line numbers for each finding
- The full PlannedWorkout type/schema definitions
- The resolver file paths that handle PlannedWorkout queries/mutations
- Any existing boolean field patterns on the model

Research the iOS app's workout preview screen and how it displays planned workouts. I need to understand:

1. Find the workout preview screen/view in the iOS codebase (likely in Modules/ somewhere). Search for files related to "WorkoutPreview", "PlannedWorkout", "WorkoutCard", or similar.
2. Find how PlannedWorkout is modeled in Swift (the GraphQL generated types and any local models).
3. Find the GraphQL schema file at `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Schema.graphql` and search for PlannedWorkout.
4. Find any GraphQL queries/fragments that fetch PlannedWorkout data.
5. Look for existing badge/icon patterns on workout cards (e.g., how different workout types are visually distinguished).
6. Check for any existing community-related UI patterns or icons in the asset catalog.

Return:
- The exact file paths and line numbers for each finding
- The full PlannedWorkout Swift type definition
- The workout preview view code (the SwiftUI view that renders workout cards/previews)
- Any existing badge/icon patterns used on workout cards
- The GraphQL fragments/queries that fetch PlannedWorkout fields

content:
Here's the complete research report:

---

## 1. GraphQL Schema Definition (Amplify/AppSync — Source of Truth for DynamoDB)

**File:** `RedefinedFitness/amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql` (lines 1–74)

```graphql
type PlannedWorkout implements Activity @shareable @apolloKey(fields: "id")
    @model(queries: null, subscriptions: null)
    @auth(rules: [
        { allow: groups, groups: ["Admins"], operations: [create, read, update, delete] }
        { allow: private, operations: [read] }
    ]) {
    id: ID!
    versionGroupID: String! @index(name: "byVersionGroupID")
    version: Int! @default(value: "1")
    isLatest: BooleanValue! @default(value: "TRUE") @index(name: "byIsLatest")
    isPublicAndLatest: String! @index(name: "byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt", sortKeyFields: ["createdAt"])
    name: String!
    time: TimeMinutes!
    type: WorkoutType! @index(name: "byWorkoutType")
    isBodyweight: Boolean!
    estimatedDurationMillis: Int
    difficulty: Difficulty!
    instructor: Instructor @belongsTo
    bodyRegions: [BodyRegion]
    goals: [WorkoutGoal]
    description: String
    muscleGroups: [MuscleGroup!]! @deprecated(reason: "Use granularMuscleGroups instead")
    granularMuscleGroups: [GranularMuscleGroup!]
    isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean
    coverImage: UserAsset
    thumbnail: UserAsset
    isChallenge: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsChallenge", sortKeyFields: ["challengeType"])
    isComplete: Boolean! @access(cognito: true) @resolver(...)
    isGenerated: Boolean @deprecated(reason: "Use isAutoGenerated instead")
    isAutoGenerated: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType", sortKeyFields: ["type"]) @default(value: "FALSE")
    isDemoWorkout: Boolean @resolver(...)
    workoutSource: WorkoutSource!
    originallyGenerated: Boolean @deprecated(reason: "Use workoutSource instead")
    challengeType: ChallengeType!
    isAmpMode: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsAmpMode", sortKeyFields: ["isLatest"])
    portraitThumbnail: UserAsset
    plannedBlocks: [PlannedBlock] @hasMany(indexName: "byPlannedWorkout", fields: ["id"])
    categories: [WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkout] @hasMany(...)
    programConnections: [ProgramWorkoutConnection] @hasMany(...)
    tips: [String] @deprecated
    benefits: [String] @deprecated
    isPublic: BooleanValue! @index(name: "byIsPublicAndCreatedAt", sortKeyFields: ["createdAt"])
    createdBy: String! @index(name: "byCreatedBy", ...) @index(name: "byCreatedByAndCreatedAt", ...) @index(name: "byCreatedByAndIsPublic", ...)
    isDraft: BooleanValue!
    isFavorite: Boolean @lambda(name: "getIsFavorite")
    isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue! @index(name: "byIsLiveChallenge")
    workoutGenerationVersion: String @index(name: "byWorkoutGenerationVersion")
    aiGenerationType: AIGenerationType
    specialWorkoutType: SpecialWorkoutType @resolver(...)
    creatorData: CreatorData @resolver(...)
    createdAt: AWSDateTime!
    isArchived: BooleanValue
    supportedOperations: [WorkoutOperation] @resolver(...)
}
```

> **Important:** This is an **Amplify `@model`** — it defines the DynamoDB table directly. There is **no separate DB schema** in `packages/amp-db/` for PlannedWorkout. The Amplify `@model` directive auto-generates the DynamoDB table, GSIs, and CRUD operations.

---

## 2. Database Schema / Table

**PlannedWorkout is NOT in `packages/amp-db/`** — it's an Amplify-managed table defined by the `@model` directive above. The table name is registered in:

**File:** `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts` (line 62)
```typescript
PlannedWorkout = 'PlannedWorkout',
```

**Data access layer:**
- **Get by ID:** `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/get-planned-workout.ts` — uses `getItem` + `queryIndex` on `byVersionGroupID` GSI to always return the latest version
- **Patch fields:** `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/patch-planned-workout.ts` — uses `patchItem<PlannedWorkout>` from `@amp/core/utils/dynamodb-utils`

---

## 3. Resolvers That Handle PlannedWorkout

### Apollo Server (Federation) — Query Resolvers
| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.getPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | `getPlannedWorkout(id: ID!): PlannedWorkout` |
| `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.queryPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | `queryPlannedWorkouts(filters, page, pageSize): QueryPlannedWorkoutsResult` |
| `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.listPersonalPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | `listPersonalPlannedWorkouts(...)` |
| `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.queryAmpModeWorkouts.resolver.ts` | AMP mode workouts |
| `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.getWorkoutRecommendationsV2.resolver.ts` | Recommendations |

### Apollo Server — Mutation Resolvers
| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `Mutation.createPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create workout (admin) |
| `Mutation.updatePlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update workout (admin) |
| `Mutation.createPersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create personal workout |
| `Mutation.updatePersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update personal workout |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutForUserV2.resolver.ts` | V2 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutV3.resolver.ts` | V3 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutLP.resolver.ts` | LP generation |
| `Mutation.applyQuickActionPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Quick actions |
| `Mutation.applyAIChangesToPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | AI changes |
| `Mutation.toggleArchived.resolver.ts` | Toggle `isArchived` |

### Apollo Server — Field Resolvers on PlannedWorkout
| File | Field |
|------|-------|
| `PlannedWorkout.calculatedPlannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `calculatedPlannedBlocks` |
| `PlannedWorkout.plannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `plannedBlocks` |
| `PlannedWorkout.isComplete.resolver.ts` | `isComplete` (computed from PerformedWorkout) |
| `PlannedWorkout.isFavorite.resolver.ts` | `isFavorite` (computed from UserFavorite) |
| `PlannedWorkout.instructor.resolver.ts` | `instructor` |
| `PlannedWorkout.creatorData.resolver.ts` | `creatorData` |
| `PlannedWorkout.type.resolver.ts` | `type` (version-gated mapping) |
| `PlannedWorkout.specialWorkoutType.resolver.ts` | `specialWorkoutType` |
| `PlannedWorkout.supportedOperations.resolver.ts` | `supportedOperations` |

### AppSync (Legacy) Resolvers
| Directory | Field |
|-----------|-------|
| `resolvers/PlannedWorkout.specialWorkoutType/` | AppSync VTL resolvers |
| `resolvers/PlannedWorkout.supportedOperations/` | AppSync VTL resolvers |

---

## 4. TypeScript Type in `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts`

**File:** `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (lines 7831–7888)

```typescript
export type PlannedWorkout = Activity & {
  __typename?: 'PlannedWorkout';
  aiGenerationType?: Maybe<AiGenerationType>;
  benefits?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  bodyRegions?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<BodyRegion>>>;
  calculatedPlannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  categories?: Maybe<ModelWorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkoutConnection>;
  challengeType: ChallengeType;
  coverImage?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  createdAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  createdBy: Scalars['String']['output'];
  creatorData?: Maybe<CreatorData>;
  description?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  difficulty: Difficulty;
  estimatedDurationMillis?: Maybe<Scalars['Int']['output']>;
  goals?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutGoal>>>;
  granularMuscleGroups?: Maybe<Array<GranularMuscleGroup>>;
  id: Scalars['ID']['output'];
  instructor?: Maybe<Instructor>;
  instructorPlannedWorkoutsId?: Maybe<Scalars['ID']['output']>;
  isAmpMode?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isArchived?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isAutoGenerated?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isBodyweight: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isChallenge?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isComplete: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isDemoWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isDraft: BooleanValue;
  isFavorite?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isLatest: BooleanValue;
  isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue;
  isOnMachineWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isPublic: BooleanValue;
  isPublicAndLatest: Scalars['String']['output'];
  muscleGroups: Array<MuscleGroup>;
  name: Scalars['String']['output'];
  originallyGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  plannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  portraitThumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  programConnections?: Maybe<ModelProgramWorkoutConnectionConnection>;
  specialWorkoutType?: Maybe<SpecialWorkoutType>;
  supportedOperations?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutOperation>>>;
  thumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  time: TimeMinutes;
  tips?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  type: WorkoutType;
  updatedAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  version: Scalars['Int']['output'];
  versionGroupID: Scalars['String']['output'];
  workoutGenerationVersion?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  workoutSource: WorkoutSource;
};
```

---

## 5. Existing Boolean Field Patterns on PlannedWorkout

There are **two distinct patterns** for boolean fields:

### Pattern A: `BooleanValue` enum (stored in DynamoDB as string `"TRUE"` / `"FALSE"`)

Used when the field needs to be a **DynamoDB GSI key** (DynamoDB doesn't support boolean keys).

| Field | Nullability | Has GSI? | Default |
|-------|-------------|----------|---------|
| `isLatest` | `BooleanValue!` (required) | ✅ `byIsLatest` | `TRUE` |
| `isPublic` | `BooleanValue!` (required) | ✅ `byIsPublicAndCreatedAt` | — |
| `isDraft` | `BooleanValue!` (required) | ❌ | — |
| `isLiveChallenge` | `BooleanValue!` (required) | ✅ `byIsLiveChallenge` | — |
| `isAutoGenerated` | `BooleanValue` (optional) | ✅ `byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType` | `FALSE` |
| `isChallenge` | `BooleanValue` (optional) | ✅ `byIsChallenge` | — |
| `isAmpMode` | `BooleanValue` (optional) | ✅ `byIsAmpMode` | — |
| `isArchived` | `BooleanValue` (optional) | ❌ | — |

**Conversion utilities** at `packages/amp-shared/src/types/boolean-value.ts`:
```typescript
booleanValueToBoolean(value: BooleanValue | null | undefined): boolean
booleanToBooleanValue(value: boolean | null | undefined): BooleanValue
```

**BooleanValue enum** defined in `amplify/.../schema/root.graphql` (line 56):
```graphql
enum BooleanValue { TRUE, FALSE }
```

### Pattern B: Native `Boolean` (stored as actual boolean in DynamoDB)

Used for **computed/resolved fields** that are NOT stored in DynamoDB, or simple stored fields that don't need GSI indexing.

| Field | Nullability | Stored in DDB? | Notes |
|-------|-------------|----------------|-------|
| `isBodyweight` | `Boolean!` (required) | ✅ | Simple stored field |
| `isOnMachineWorkout` | `Boolean` (optional) | ✅ | Simple stored field |
| `isComplete` | `Boolean!` (required) | ❌ | **Computed** — resolved via field resolver checking PerformedWorkout |
| `isFavorite` | `Boolean` (optional) | ❌ | **Computed** — resolved via Lambda checking UserFavorite |
| `isDemoWorkout` | `Boolean` (optional) | ❌ | **Computed** — resolved via VTL resolver |
| `isGenerated` | `Boolean` (optional) | ✅ | Deprecated |

### Pattern C: The `isArchived` pattern (most recent addition — best to follow)

**`isArchived`** is the most recently added boolean field and represents the cleanest pattern:

- **Schema:** `isArchived: BooleanValue` (optional, no GSI, no default)
- **Mutation:** `toggleArchived(id: ID!, isArchived: Boolean!): Boolean!` in `root.graphql` line 182
- **Resolver:** `Mutation.toggleArchived.resolver.ts` — uses `patchPlannedWorkoutField()` with `booleanToBooleanValue()` conversion
- **No field resolver needed** — the value is stored directly in DDB and returned as-is

This is the pattern to follow for a new boolean field that:
1. Is stored in DynamoDB (not computed)
2. Doesn't need a GSI
3. Uses `BooleanValue` enum type for consistency with the rest of the model

COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- RESEARCH_GRAPHQL_SCHEMA: PASS Found in amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql (lines 1-74)
- RESEARCH_DB_SCHEMA: PASS PlannedWorkout is Amplify @model managed (no amp-db entry). Table name in table-names.ts line 62
- RESEARCH_RESOLVERS: PASS Found 30+ resolver files across Apollo Server and AppSync
- RESEARCH_API_TS: PASS Found at packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts lines 7831-7888
- RESEARCH_BOOLEAN_PATTERNS: PASS Identified 3 patterns (BooleanValue enum, native Boolean, isArchived as best reference)
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none — research only)
BLOCKED: NO
name: task

content:
Research is now complete. Here's a comprehensive summary of everything discovered:

---

## Complete Research Summary: Workout Preview Screen

### 1. Workout Details Screen Architecture

**Entry point:** `WorkoutDetailsViewModel` (MVVM pattern with Coordinator navigation)
- Context enum supports: `.id(String)`, `.workout(PlannedWorkout)`, `.fullWorkout(PlannedWorkout)`, `.aiWorkout(...)`, `.quickActionWorkout(...)`, `.aiQuickActionWorkout(...)`
- Actions delegate to Coordinator: dismiss, edit, startWorkout, movementDetails, swap, share, etc.

**View:** `WorkoutDetailsViewController` — UITableView with DiffableDataSource
- Single section (`.main`) with `WorkoutDetailsItem` items
- Nav bar: back, share, favorite, edit buttons
- Bottom: `WorkoutDetailsStartWorkoutButton` (start button + optional "Workout Together" button)

**State mapping:** `WorkoutDetailsStateMapper` maps `PlannedWorkout` → `WorkoutDetailsViewData`

**Screen layout (top to bottom):**
1. **`.header`** — Stretchable cover image with parallax, badges overlay (bottom-left: `LibraryBadgesView` with all titles visible), duration badge (bottom-right)
2. **`.title`** — Workout name + up to 3 muscle group icons
3. **`.voiceMessage`** — Conditional AI voice message player
4. **`.creator`** — Instructor avatar + name + social media icon (or BYO creator name). Hidden for challenges and when voice message visible
5. **`.description`** — Workout description text (or voice message text)
6. **`.quickActions`** — Quick action buttons (conditional)
7. **`.muscles`** — Front/back muscle diagram with primary/secondary muscle captions
8. **`.workoutPlanHeader`/`.workoutPlanItem`** — Block-by-block workout plan (hidden for challenges)
9. **`.archive`** — Archive button (conditional on `supportedOperations`)

### 2. Badge System

**`BadgeStyle` enum** (in `AmpUI`): 18 styles including:
- **Community:** icon = `ampPerson2` (person.2), title = "Community", white foreground, blur background
- **Challenge:** icon = `ampWorkoutChallenge`, title from L10n
- **New:** sparkle icon, white foreground, blur background
- **Favorite:** bookmark icon
- **Completed:** checkmark icon
- **Generated:** sparkles icon (AI-generated)
- **PlannedWorkoutDay:** yellow background (#FFCB5C), black text, no icon
- **ActiveRecoveryDay:** blue background (#92D5FF), black text, no icon

**`LibraryBadgesView`** (in `AmpLibrary`): Reusable badge row component
- Renders in order: generated → challenge → community → new (if not generated/completed) → favorite → completed
- Each badge has a `TitleVisible` boolean for showing/hiding text labels
- Used by: `LibraryWorkoutCardView`, `LibraryWorkoutRowView`, `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`

### 3. `isCommunityWorkout` Flow

**GraphQL:** `PlannedWorkout.isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field
- Present in `PlannedWorkoutBase` fragment (NOT in `PlannedWorkoutMinimal`)
- Available in Full/FullWithArguments fragments

**Swift model:** `PlannedWorkout.isCommunityWorkout: Bool?` (optional)
- Set from `data.isCommunityWorkout == true` in base initializer

**UI propagation:**
- `LibraryWorkoutViewData.isCommunityWorkout` → `LibraryWorkoutRowView` / `LibraryWorkoutCardView`
- `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewData.isCommunityWorkout` → `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`
- **NOT present** in `WorkoutCardViewData` (Home screen weekly carousel)

### 4. Community-Related UI

- **Community badge:** `BadgeView(style: .community(withTitle: true/false))` — person.2 icon + "Community" text
- **Workout Together button:** Lottie animation button next to Start Workout, controlled by `isWorkoutTogetherEnabled` feature flag
- **Workout Together Unavailable view:** Sheet with machine image + person.2 icon overlay, explaining feature unavailability
- **`ampPerson2` icon:** Only used in community badge and WorkoutTogetherUnavailableView

### 5. Key Data Models

| Model | Location | Key Fields |
|-------|----------|------------|
| `PlannedWorkout` | AmpModels | id, name, time, type, difficulty, instructor, muscleGroups, coverImage, isCommunityWorkout, etc. |
| `WorkoutDetailsViewData` | Amp target | items: [WorkoutDetailsItem], title, isEditEnabled |
| `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewData` | Amp target | isNew, isGenerated, isCompleted, isChallenge, isCommunityWorkout, thumbnail, durationDescription |
| `WorkoutDetailsTitleCellViewData` | Amp target | name, mainMuscleGroups |
| `WorkoutDetailsCreatorSectionViewData` | Amp target | name (Name), thumbnail, socialMediaPlatform |
| `LibraryWorkoutViewData` | AmpLibrary | id, name, type, bodyRegion, image, duration, isCommunityWorkout, etc. |
| `WorkoutCardViewData` | AmpHome | id, title, description, duration, workoutType, coverImage (no isCommunityWorkout) |
| `MusclesViewData` | AmpUI | front/back primary/secondary muscles, bodyType, captions |
| `WorkoutPlanBlockViewData` | AmpUI | id, title, state, movementsViewData, movementActions |

```
COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- SCHEMA_PULL: N_A (research-only task, no schema changes)
- COMPILE: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- LINT: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- UNIT_TESTS: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- SNAPSHOT_TESTS: N_A (research-only task, no code changes)
- MAESTRO: SKIPPED (research-only task, no UI changes)
- SCREENSHOTS: none
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none — research only)
BLOCKED: NO
BLOCK_REASON: N/A
```
name: task

content.text: Excellent research results. The key finding is that **`isCommunityWorkout` already exists in the iOS app** — the badge system, Swift model, view data, and UI components are all already wired up. The missing piece is the **backend schema field**. Let me verify this.

content.text:
This is very revealing. The iOS app **already has full support** for `isCommunityWorkout` — the Swift model, GraphQL fragments, badge UI, and all view data propagation are already in place. The iOS schema file even already has the field defined at line 6532 of `Schema.graphql`.

However, the **backend GraphQL schema** (`planned-workout.graphql`) does **NOT** have `isCommunityWorkout`. This means the field exists in the iOS-side schema (likely added speculatively) but the backend never actually defines or returns it.

The task is primarily a **backend-only change**: add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the PlannedWorkout type in the backend schema, run codegen, and verify. The iOS side is already complete.

Let me verify the iOS schema to confirm:

Now let me create the plan:

input.title: Add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout model
input.summary: Backend-only change needed. iOS already has full support for isCommunityWorkout (model, fragments, badge UI). Just need to add the field to the backend GraphQL schema and run codegen.
input.plan:
## Implementation Plan

### Key Finding
The iOS app **already fully supports** `isCommunityWorkout`:
- `Schema.graphql` line 6532: `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean`
- `PlannedWorkoutBase` fragment fetches it
- `PlannedWorkout.swift` model has `isCommunityWorkout: Bool?`
- `BadgeStyle.community` renders a person.2 icon with "Community" text
- `LibraryBadgesView`, `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`, `LibraryWorkoutCardView`, `LibraryWorkoutRowView` all display the badge
- `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewModel` maps `workout.isCommunityWorkout == true` to the header view data

The **only missing piece** is the backend schema definition.

### Backend Changes (RedefinedFitness)

1. **Add `isCommunityWorkout` field to PlannedWorkout GraphQL schema**
   - File: `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
   - Add: `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` (optional, no GSI needed, no default)
   - Place it after `isArchived: BooleanValue` (line 69), following the pattern of simple stored boolean fields like `isBodyweight` and `isOnMachineWorkout`

2. **Run `pnpm generate`** to regenerate:
   - `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (TypeScript types)
   - AppSync schema compilation
   - Any other codegen artifacts

3. **Run `pnpm typecheck`** to verify no type errors

### iOS Changes
**None required** — iOS already has complete support for this field.

### Test Plan
- [ ] Verify `pnpm generate` succeeds and `isCommunityWorkout` appears in generated `API.ts`
- [ ] Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] Verify `pnpm lint` passes

content:
Here's the plan:

**Key finding:** The iOS app already has **complete support** for `isCommunityWorkout` — the Swift model, GraphQL fragments, badge UI (person.2 icon + "Community" text), and all view data propagation are already wired up. The only missing piece is the **backend GraphQL schema definition**.

### Plan
1. **Backend only**: Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the `PlannedWorkout` type in `planned-workout.graphql`
2. Run `pnpm generate` to update generated TypeScript types
3. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` to verify

No iOS changes needed — the iOS side is already complete and will start displaying community badges as soon as the backend returns the field.

Shall I proceed with implementation?

content:
I've posted the plan to the dashboard. The plan is:

**Backend-only change** — Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the PlannedWorkout GraphQL schema, then run codegen and typecheck.

**No iOS changes needed** — The iOS app already has complete support: the Swift model (`PlannedWorkout.isCommunityWorkout: Bool?`), GraphQL fragments (`PlannedWorkoutBase`), badge UI (`BadgeStyle.community` with person.2 icon + "Community" text), and all view data propagation in `LibraryBadgesView`, `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell`, `LibraryWorkoutCardView`, and `LibraryWorkoutRowView`.

Waiting for your approval to proceed with implementation.
